business_description: Retail stores specializing in sale of wireless products, wireless services, and accessories, including smartphones, tablets, watches, accessories, wireless home internet, and other services/products associated with devices.

item1.founded: July 1, 1988 as Automotive Technologies, Inc. (CT corp). Converted to CT LLC October 2016.

affiliates.name: The Cellular Connection, LLC (TCC)
affiliates.description: Owns stores under 'TCC' mark offering similar goods/services; may compete with WZ franchisees

affiliates.name: The Culture of Good, Inc.
affiliates.description: Management and business practices consulting services

affiliates.name: Round Room Gives, Inc.
affiliates.description: Charitable organization; franchisees required to contribute $0.25 per activation/upgrade

item5.fee_refundable: Non-refundable except possible partial refund if site not found in 180 days and franchisor terminates
item5.vet_fran_discount: 50% off standard fee (veteran must be 51%+ owner)

item6.royalty_structure: 22% on first $5,000 GP/month, decreasing in $5,000 increments to 9% on GP above $100,000/month
item6.royalty_collection: Deducted from Provider Commissions/Residuals before payout; deficiency by 10th of month
item6.ad_fund_rate: No mandatory ad fund from franchisees; co-op funds from Provider at franchisor's discretion

item10.third_party_lender: Ascentium Capital LLC (division of Regions Bank)
item10.security_interest: All franchisee assets (equipment, inventory, franchise business)

item11.pos_equipment: 2 desktop POS + 4 mobile POS tablets + 2 credit card readers + printer + network/security equipment

item17.non_compete_during_term: No involvement in competing wireless/wireline business
item17.dispute_resolution: Mediation first (neutral location), then arbitration within 15 miles of Indianapolis, IN
item17.franchisor_purchase_option: Gross Profits for trailing 12 months + FMV of tangible assets

item19.population_description: Franchised stores open full 12 months of 2024

concluded.case: Family Wireless #1, LLC et al. v. Automotive Technologies, Inc.
concluded.summary: 32 franchisees challenged royalty calculation on Provider commissions. Settled: avg $48,440/location, revised multi-store royalty program.

concluded.case: Tel Group, LLC et al. v Automotive Technologies, Inc.
concluded.summary: 2 franchisees (25 stores) with similar royalty claims plus renewal rights dispute. Settled: $1,250,000 collective payment, revised incentive program for 10 years.

special_risks.description: Mediation at neutral location selected by mediator; arbitration and litigation only in Indiana. May force less favorable settlement and increase costs.

minimum_gp_buyout_trigger.detail: Failure to achieve $60,000 GP in any calendar year triggers franchisor buyout right (FA Section 13.04.B.8)

item17_depth_additions.abandonment_definition: Ceasing to operate 5 separate business days in any 12-month period or 3 consecutive days (FA Section 13.04.B.4)
item17_depth_additions.business_judgment_clause: Franchisor's decisions may not be second-guessed by any court/trier of fact, even when other alternatives are reasonable (FA Section 18.05)
item17_depth_additions.innovation_assignment: All franchisee innovations, improvements, advertising ideas become franchisor's property (FA Section 2.01.B)
item17_depth_additions.purchase_price_formula: 1x trailing 12-month GP + FMV of tangible assets (equipment at cost less 2%/month depreciation; materials/supplies/inventory at cost) (FA Section 13.04.A)

unresolveds.description: Provider identity not disclosed in FDD
Auditor firm name not captured from text layer (letterhead image only)
Effective aggregate royalty rate not calculable from FDD alone (sliding scale + incentive programs)
No store-level profitability data — operating expenses, rent, payroll excluded from Item 19 FPR
Minimum monthly activation thresholds not disclosed (published in Operations Manual only)
Terms offered to 277 TCC conversion franchisees (2023) vs. standard terms not disclosed
Distribution sustainability — distributions consistently exceed net income, accumulated deficit growing ($46.8M)

contradictions.description: Item 20 Table 1 shows 447 at end of 2022; Table 3 total shows 446 — minor 1-store discrepancy
Item 6 states royalty range 9%-22% of GP; Note 2 to financials states 5%-22%. The 5% reflects incentive-adjusted minimum.
contradictions.resolution_status: Resolved — 5% is after-incentive floor, 9% is pre-incentive minimum

contradictions.description: Item 1 states no company-owned stores; Item 12 reserves right to operate stores — consistent but noteworthy for encroachment risk
contradictions.resolution_status: No contradiction — different scope (current vs. reserved rights)
